mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of rate-decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances where unemployment rises by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital investment demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative forward returns in more than 55% of cases or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A declining index of industrial production reflects contracting manufacturing output and reduced corporate revenue expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in more than 65% of periods following a month-over-month decline in industrial production.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of months where CPI inflation exceeds the 0.4% monthly threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening and wage growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive returns with a rank IC above 0.04 over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real GDP and rising personal consumption expenditures during a low-interest-rate environment amplify corporate earnings growth, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 50% of observations when real GDP growth exceeds 2% annually and rates remain below 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive months of declining industrial production signal a manufacturing contraction that rapidly transmits to equity valuations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in more than 60% of periods following two consecutive months of industrial production declines.
